Ad Text:
An exciting opportunity for an Operational Technology Cyber Security Business Analyst to join a public sector organisation in Brisbane. You'll lead discovery, requirements, and gap analysis activities across OT and cyber initiatives, working with diverse stakeholders to deliver secure, resilient solutions that support critical infrastructure.

An exciting opportunity is available for a highly skilled Operational Technology Cyber Security Business Analyst to join a forward‑thinking public sector organisation in Brisbane. This pivotal role is ideal for someone who enjoys delivering meaningful outcomes by shaping technology initiatives across OT and cyber security environments.

You will be central to initiatives that enhance security, efficiency, and compliance across critical infrastructure, working closely with internal stakeholders and delivery partners to support seamless OT and cyber security implementations. The organisation offers a supportive, inclusive workplace with flexible working arrangements and ongoing professional development, enabling you to grow your career while making a genuine impact.

Why join?

Play a key role in technology initiatives that strengthen OT and cyber security across essential services
Enjoy flexible working arrangements that support work-life balance
Collaborate with experienced, values-driven teams in a supportive and inclusive environment
What you'll do

As an OT Cyber Security Business Analyst, you will play a central role in the success of initiatives that underpin critical operations. You will lead structured discovery activities, engage stakeholders across the organisation, and translate business needs into clear, testable requirements aligned with industry best practice.

Key responsibilities include:

Leading discovery sessions to understand current-state processes, systems, roles, responsibilities, and constraints within OT and cyber environments
Conducting detailed gap analyses against standards such as IS18 and ISO27000, identifying risks and opportunities for improvement
Facilitating stakeholder engagement through interviews and workshops to gather and validate requirements
Developing comprehensive business cases outlining problems, benefits, costs, risks, and delivery options
Preparing accurate and complete project initiation artefacts aligned to strategic and operational objectives
Producing prioritised, traceable, and testable requirements documentation to support internal teams and third‑party vendors
Maintaining robust requirements traceability from initiation through to solution acceptance
Supporting testing, acceptance, and transition into business‑as‑usual operations
Reviewing and redesigning processes and workflows to improve security, resilience, and long‑term sustainability

Your proactive approach and attention to detail will help identify issues early, support continuous improvement, and ensure secure, future‑ready outcomes.

What you bring

You will bring strong experience as a Business Analyst within operational technology and/or cyber security environments, with a proven ability to translate complex needs into practical, value-driven solutions.

Key skills and experience include:

Demonstrated experience leading structured discovery activities in OT or cyber security settings
Proven capability performing gap analyses against IS18, ISO27000, or similar frameworks
Strong stakeholder engagement skills, including facilitating workshops across multidisciplinary teams
Experience developing well-structured business cases for executive decision-making
High attention to detail when producing project initiation and requirements documentation
Advanced capability in producing testable, traceable requirements across the project lifecycle
Experience working with vendors and maintaining alignment through traceability frameworks
Ability to provide clear artefacts such as process maps, acceptance criteria, and requirements specifications
A proactive, collaborative mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and risk mitigation
Strong process analysis and redesign skills to enhance operational efficiency, security, and resilience



Robert Walters Australia is acting as an Employment Business in relation to this vacancy.

Company: Sunshine Coast Hospital and Health Service
Ad Text:
We're excited to welcome a Principal Data Analyst – Scientific Analysis, who performs applied analytical investigations to inform operational and clinical decision-making across Sunshine Coast Hospital and Health Service.

The role works with clinicians, managers and executives to clarify problems, translate them into structured analytical approaches, and deliver evidence-based findings that support action.

Sunshine Coast Health is a values-based organisation. We are committed to the maintenance of a culture in which we treat our colleagues with respect as we do our patients, consumers and the community. We respect, protect and promote human rights in our decision-making and actions.

Key Responsibilities include:

Engage with clinicians, managers and stakeholders to clarify operational and clinical problems and translate them into structured analytical approaches and work plans, for example understanding drivers of overcrowding, delays to discharge or variation in care pathways.
Plan and lead defined analytical work packages and small projects, managing scope, timelines and deliverables within a busy operational environment.
Undertake complex data extraction, preparation and analysis using SQL and analytical programming languages such as R or Python.

Remuneration value up to $171,568 p.a., comprising salary between $140,115 - $150,371 p.a., employer contribution to superannuation (12.75%) and annual leave loading (17.5%) (iAO7). Applications will remain current for 12 months. Please note: only applications from candidates will be accepted (applications that may result in an agency fee will not be considered).

Why work for us?

We offer rewarding career opportunities across a wide range of clinical and non-clinical areas across our health service and pride ourselves on providing a work environment that is safe, satisfying, flexible, and promotes a healthy work-life balance.

As a Queensland Health employee, you will benefit from a higher than standard employer contribution to Superannuation of up to 12.75%, access to salary packaging, flexible working arrangements and competitive salary rates with annual incremental increases.

We are committed to providing a diverse and inclusive workplace for our people and our community. We encourage people of all genders, races, ages and abilities to apply for roles within our Health Service.

You can find out more about why it's so great to work at Sunshine Coast here: https://www.health.qld.gov.au/sunshinecoast/careers

As a Sunshine Coast Health employee, you will receive competitive remuneration and have access to:

A wellness program;
Generous superannuation;
Flexible work arrangements;
Career training and development; and
Salary packaging.
